ucs.title: [LLM] Qilin/Agenda Chrome credential-harvesting GPO script (IPScanner.ps1) on domain endpoints
ucs.description: Hunts Qilin's documented July 2024 tradecraft (Sophos X-Ops): after compromising a domain controller, the affiliate pushes a logon script via GPO that copies Chrome 'Login Data'/'Cookies' on every domain-joined machine and stages the database for offline DPAPI cracking. The script (commonly IPScanner.ps1 / logon.bat) is invoked from SYSVOL/NETLOGON by gpscript.exe, which is a high-fidelity Qilin signature distinct from generic Mimikatz/LSASS dumping.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("gpscript.exe","userinit.exe","explorer.exe","svchost.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe") AND (Processes.process LIKE "%\\SYSVOL\\%" OR Processes.process LIKE "%\\NETLOGON\\%" OR Processes.process LIKE "%IPScanner%" OR Processes.process LIKE "%Chrome\\User Data%" OR Processes.process LIKE "%Login Data%" OR Processes.process LIKE "%\\Local State%" OR Processes.process LIKE "%logon.bat%")) by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where match(user,"(?i).+") AND NOT match(user,"(?i).+\$$") | rename host as dest | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let _gpoPaths = dynamic([@"\sysvol\", @"\netlogon\", @"\policies\\{"]);
let _chromeArtifacts = dynamic([@"Chrome\User Data", "Login Data", "Local State", "Web Data", @"Edge\User Data", "IPScanner"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe")
| where (InitiatingProcessParentFileName =~ "gpscript.exe"             // GPO logon-script execution
         or InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any (_gpoPaths)
         or ProcessCommandLine has_any (_gpoPaths))
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (_chromeArtifacts)
   or ProcessCommandLine has_all ("Copy-Item","AppData")
   or ProcessCommandLine has "IPScanner"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          GrandParent   = InitiatingProcessParentFileName,
          ScriptHost    = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd     = InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _gpoPaths = dynamic([@"\sysvol\", @"\netlogon\", @"\policies\\{"]);
let _chromeArtifacts = dynamic([@"Chrome\User Data", "Login Data", "Local State", "Web Data", @"Edge\User Data", "IPScanner"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
| where EventID == 4688                                                  // requires cmdline auditing
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName has_any ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe")
| where ParentProcessName has "gpscript.exe"
     or CommandLine has_any (_gpoPaths)
| where CommandLine has_any (_chromeArtifacts)
     or CommandLine has "IPScanner"
     or (CommandLine has "Copy-Item" and CommandLine has "AppData")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Qilin/Agenda GPO-Pushed Chrome Credential Harvester
id: a8d4b2e6-9c17-4f8b-93b7-0c6d9bf5e211
status: experimental
description: Detects PowerShell/cmd/wscript invoked from a SYSVOL/NETLOGON GPO logon script that touches Chrome/Edge credential stores - the Qilin IPScanner.ps1 pattern reported by Sophos X-Ops in July 2024.
references:
  - https://news.sophos.com/en-us/2024/08/22/qilin-ransomware-caught-stealing-credentials-stored-in-google-chrome/
author: Detection Engineering
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_proc:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
  selection_gpo:
    - ParentImage|endswith: '\gpscript.exe'
    - CommandLine|contains:
        - '\SYSVOL\'
        - '\NETLOGON\'
        - '\Policies\{'
  selection_chrome:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'Chrome\User Data'
      - 'Login Data'
      - 'Local State'
      - 'Web Data'
      - 'Edge\User Data'
      - 'IPScanner'
  filter_machine:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_proc and selection_gpo and selection_chrome and not filter_machine
fields:
  - Image
  - ParentImage
  - CommandLine
  - User
  - Computer
falsepositives:
  - Sanctioned password-rotation tooling distributed via GPO
level: high
ucs.rationale: Generic 'PowerShell touches Login Data' fires on Stealc/RedLine/etc. The Qilin signature is the *delivery vector*: gpscript.exe (Group Policy logon script processor) executing PS/cmd from a SYSVOL/NETLOGON path that immediately reads Chrome credential artefacts. That chain only happens when an attacker who already owns a DC weaponises GPO to fan out credential theft to every domain workstation - w
_sigma_issues: no MITRE ATT&CK tag (expected at least one `attack.t####`)

ucs.title: [LLM] Qilin/Agenda safe-mode reboot pre-encryption (bcdedit safeboot + RunOnce + shadow deletion)
ucs.description: Hunts the Agenda/Qilin Rust-variant signature behaviour: the operator reconfigures the boot loader to safe-mode-with-networking via bcdedit, registers an autorun (RunOnce or service) so the encryptor runs after reboot when most EDR/AV are not loaded, then deletes shadow copies and forces a reboot. Trend Micro and Group-IB attribute this evasion sequence specifically to Qilin/Agenda - few other ransomware families chain bcdedit safeboot with autorun seeding.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("bcdedit.exe","vssadmin.exe","wmic.exe","wbadmin.exe","shutdown.exe","powershell.exe","reg.exe") AND ( (Processes.process_name="bcdedit.exe" AND Processes.process LIKE "%safeboot%") OR (Processes.process_name="vssadmin.exe" AND Processes.process LIKE "%delete%shadows%") OR (Processes.process_name="wmic.exe" AND Processes.process LIKE "%shadowcopy%delete%") OR (Processes.process_name="wbadmin.exe" AND Processes.process LIKE "%delete%catalog%") OR (Processes.process_name="reg.exe" AND Processes.process LIKE "%RunOnce%") OR (Processes.process_name="shutdown.exe" AND Processes.process LIKE "%/r%") ) by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | bin _time span=30m | stats min(_time) as firstSeen max(_time) as lastSeen values(process) as cmdlines values(process_name) as tools dc(process_name) as toolCount by host user _time | where toolCount >= 2 AND mvfind(tools,"bcdedit.exe") >= 0 | rename host as dest
ucs.kql:
let _window = 30m;
let _bcd = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has "safeboot"
         and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("minimal","network")
    | project bcdTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              BcdCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              BcdParent = InitiatingProcessFileName;
let _support = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where (FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("delete","shadows"))
         or (FileName =~ "wmic.exe"     and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("shadowcopy","delete"))
         or (FileName =~ "wbadmin.exe"  and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("delete","catalog"))
         or (FileName =~ "reg.exe"      and ProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\RunOnce", @"\Run\"))
         or (FileName =~ "shutdown.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/r","-r"))
         or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
             and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Restart-Computer","Win32_Shadowcopy"))
    | project supTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, SupTool = FileName, SupCmd = ProcessCommandLine;
_bcd
| join kind=inner _support on DeviceId
| where supTime between (bcdTime - _window .. bcdTime + _window)
| summarize SupportTools = make_set(SupTool),
            SampleSupCmds = make_set(SupCmd, 5),
            FirstSeen = min(bcdTime), LastSeen = max(supTime)
            by DeviceName, AccountName, BcdCmd, BcdParent
| where array_length(SupportTools) >= 1   // bcdedit + at least one ransomware-prep tool
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _window = 30m;
let _bcd = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where NewProcessName endswith "bcdedit.exe"
    | where CommandLine has "safeboot" and CommandLine has_any ("minimal","network")
    | project bcdTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              BcdCmd = CommandLine, BcdParent = ParentProcessName;
let _support = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where (NewProcessName endswith "vssadmin.exe" and CommandLine has_all ("delete","shadows"))
         or (NewProcessName endswith "wmic.exe"     and CommandLine has_all ("shadowcopy","delete"))
         or (NewProcessName endswith "wbadmin.exe"  and CommandLine has_all ("delete","catalog"))
         or (NewProcessName endswith "reg.exe"      and CommandLine has_any (@"\RunOnce", @"\Run\"))
         or (NewProcessName endswith "shutdown.exe" and CommandLine has_any ("/r","-r"))
         or (NewProcessName has_any ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
             and CommandLine has_any ("Restart-Computer","Win32_Shadowcopy"))
    | project supTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, SupTool = NewProcessName, SupCmd = CommandLine;
_bcd
| join kind=inner _support on Computer
| where supTime between (bcdTime - _window .. bcdTime + _window)
| summarize SupportTools = make_set(SupTool),
            SampleSupCmds = make_set(SupCmd, 5),
            FirstSeen = min(bcdTime), LastSeen = max(supTime)
            by Computer, Account, BcdCmd, BcdParent
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.rationale: bcdedit /set safeboot is one of the lowest-volume cmdline patterns in any enterprise estate (legitimate use is a sysadmin in WinRE, single-digit per month). Pairing it with a ±30 minute window of vssadmin/wmic shadow deletion, RunOnce registration, or forced reboot collapses to almost zero baseline noise but matches the Agenda/Qilin Rust-variant evasion sequence reported by Trend Micro and Group-I
